content:
my-course/
├── course.json
└── topics/
    ├── 01-introduction/
    │   ├── content.json
    │   ├── intro.md
    │   └── hello.py
    ├── 02-variables-and-types/
    │   ├── content.json
    │   ├── overview.md
    │   ├── example.py
    │   └── diagram.png
    └── 03-functions/
        ├── content.json
        └── explanation.md
label: Full course folder tree

body: The `course.json` file and the `topics/` directory must both live at the root of the course folder. The app will not search subdirectories for them.

Topic folder names must start with a numeric prefix followed by a hyphen (e.g. `01-`). Folders without a numeric prefix will not be recognised. The kebab-case portion after the prefix becomes the display title, so choose descriptive, readable names.

question: Given a topic folder named `04-loops-and-iteration`, what display title will the app generate?
explanation: The numeric prefix and its trailing hyphen are stripped, the remaining hyphens are replaced with spaces, and the result is title-cased. So `04-loops-and-iteration` becomes "Loops And Iteration".

content:
03-functions/
├── content.json      (required — defines the block sequence)
├── explanation.md    (referenced by a text block)
├── examples.md       (referenced by another text block)
├── factorial.py      (referenced by a code block)
└── diagram.png       (referenced by an image block)
label: Anatomy of a topic folder

body: All `src` paths in `content.json` are relative to the topic folder. You cannot use `../` to reference files outside the topic directory — the loader will reject path traversal for security reasons.

question: Which of the following is the only required file inside a topic folder?
explanation: Every topic folder must contain a `content.json` file. This JSON array defines the ordered sequence of blocks for the topic. All other files (markdown, code, images) are optional and only needed if referenced by blocks.

question: Where must the `topics/` directory be located?
options: At the root of the course folder, alongside course.json
It is optional — topics can be placed directly in the root
explanation: The `topics/` directory must sit at the root of the course folder, right next to `course.json`. The loader looks for it in exactly that location.

question: A course has three topics: an introduction to HTML, a topic on CSS selectors, and a topic on responsive design. Which set of folder names follows the naming convention?
explanation: Each folder needs a numeric prefix for ordering (01, 02, 03) followed by a hyphen and a kebab-case description. The exact wording may vary, but the pattern should be consistent: number-hyphen-lowercase-words-separated-by-hyphens.
